nodes.context: Mexican criminal organization and U.S.-designated Foreign Terrorist Organization that splintered from Los Zetas. Operates mainly in Nuevo Laredo and several Mexican states, with presence in the US, Guatemala, Honduras, and Colombia.
Mexican drug cartel from which Cártel del Noreste splintered. Formerly led by Omar Treviño Morales.
Gang that controlled Nuevo Laredo and was later absorbed by the Gulf Cartel in 2001. The Treviño Morales family had a relationship with them.
Mexican drug cartel that absorbed Los Texas and employed Miguel Ángel Treviño Morales. Aligned with Zetas Vieja Escuela.
Faction of Los Zetas led by original members, aligned with the Gulf Cartel and rival to Cártel del Noreste.
Mexican drug cartel listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.
Faction of the Juárez Cartel, listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.
Mexican drug cartel listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.

edges.context: Cártel del Noreste splintered from Los Zetas after the capture of Omar Treviño Morales.
Los Texas was absorbed by the Gulf Cartel in 2001.
Zetas Vieja Escuela is aligned with the Gulf Cartel.
Cártel del Noreste and Zetas Vieja Escuela are rival factions that formed after the split of Los Zetas.
Cártel del Noreste is listed as a rival of the Gulf Cartel.
CJNG is listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.
La Línea is listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.
Sinaloa Cartel is listed as a rival of Cártel del Noreste.
